context: Evaluate the agent's ability to identify bashisms in a POSIX sh script and produce a portable replacement

checklist.name: declare -a identified as bashism
checklist.description: Agent identifies that declare -a is bash-specific and not available in POSIX sh; explains that POSIX sh has no arrays

checklist.name: local identified as bashism
checklist.description: Agent identifies that local is a bash extension (not in POSIX sh spec) and suggests removing it or restructuring the function

checklist.name: [[ ]] identified as bashism
checklist.description: Agent identifies that [[ $? -eq 0 ]] and [[ ${#FAILED_TESTS[@]} -gt 0 ]] use bash double-bracket syntax and replaces them with [ ] or equivalent POSIX constructs

checklist.name: Array operations replaced with POSIX alternative
checklist.description: Agent replaces FAILED_TESTS+=(...) and ${FAILED_TESTS[@]} array operations with a POSIX-compatible alternative (e.g., space-separated string, temp file, or counter-only approach)

checklist.name: Corrected POSIX sh script produced
checklist.description: Agent produces a complete corrected script that uses only POSIX sh features; the shebang remains #!/bin/sh

checklist.name: Each change explained
checklist.description: Agent provides an explanation for each substitution made, referencing why the original construct fails on dash/POSIX sh
